2604.14779.title: AIM: Asymmetric Information Masking for Visual Question Answering Continual Learning
2604.14779.paper_summary: In continual visual question answering (VQA), existing Continual Learning (CL) methods are mostly built for symmetric, unimodal architectures. However, modern Vision-Language Models (VLMs) violate this assumption, as their trainable components are inherently asymmetric. This structural mismatch renders VLMs highly prone to catastrophic forgetting when learning from continuous data streams. Specifically, the asymmetry causes standard global regularization to favor the massive language decoder during optimization, leaving the smaller but critical visual projection layers highly vulnerable to interference. Consequently, this localized degradation leads to a severe loss of compositional reasoning capabilities. To address this, we propose Asymmetric Information Masking (AIM), which balances stability and plasticity by applying targeted masks based on modality-specific sensitivity. Experiments on VQA v2 and GQA under continual VQA settings show that AIM achieves state-of-the-art performance in both Average Performance (AP) and Average Forgetting (AF), while better preserving generalization to novel skill-concept compositions.
2604.14779.paper_summary_zh: 在持續視覺問題回答（VQA）中，現有的持續學習（CL）方法大多是為對稱的單模態架構而建立的。<lb/>然而，現代視覺-語言模型（VLMs）違反了這一假設，因為它們的可訓練組件本質上是不對稱的。<lb/>這種結構不匹配使得 VLMs 在從持續數據流中學習時高度容易遭受災難性遺忘。<lb/>具體而言，不對稱性使得標準的全局正則化在優化過程中偏向於龐大的語言解碼器，從而使得較小但關鍵的視覺投影層高度易受干擾。<lb/>因此，這種局部退化導致了組合推理能力的嚴重損失。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了不對稱信息遮罩（AIM），通過根據模態特定的敏感性應用有針對性的遮罩來平衡穩定性和可塑性。<lb/>在持續 VQA 設置下對 VQA v2 和 GQA 的實驗顯示，AIM 在平均性能（AP）和平均遺忘（AF）方面都達到了最先進的性能，同時更好地保持了對新技能-概念組合的泛化能力。
2604.14779.author: Peifeng Zhang et.al.
2604.14779.authors: Peifeng Zhang, Zice Qiu, Donghua Yu, Shilei Cao, Juepeng Zheng, Yutong Lu, Haohuan Fu
